authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
Eldorado National Forest, USDA Forest Service

sources.title: Union Valley Reservoir — Wikipedia
sources.quotedText: The reservoir is fed by Big Silver Creek, Jones Fork Silver Creek, Tells Creek, Wench Creek and the outflows of Robbs Peak and Jones Fork powerhouses.

sources.title: Eldorado National Forest : Recreation Site — Wench Creek Campground
CCR T14 §5.85(a)(2) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland streams, rivers, and canals)
sources.quotedText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Wench Creek not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Wench Creek not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 07/10/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions.

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85 — Winter catch-and-release period for general streams/rivers
sources.quotedText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

end.verbatim: the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April

parameters.note: Wench Creek is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide stream/river regulation in §5.85(a)(2), including its winter zero-bag catch-and-release period.

regulations.humanSummary: Open the last Saturday in April through November 15 (general statewide stream/river regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water); November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April is a zero-trout, artificial-lures-with-barbless-hooks period
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland streams, rivers, and canals: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, last Saturday in April through November 15.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit, last Saturday in April through November 15
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions.

parameters.counting_rule: Zero-trout bag limit (catch-and-release only) from November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April; trout must be released unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero-trout bag limit (catch-and-release for trout), November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April
regulations.verbatimText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.note: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April
regulations.verbatimText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
